etymology.description: 十 (contraction of 盾(盾) (dùn, "shield")) + 口(口) (kǒu, "mouth") = original form of 固(固) (gù, "solid")
etymology.interpretation: The character 古 (gǔ), meaning 'ancient,' originally combined the concept of a shield represented by 十, contracted from 盾(盾) (dùn, "shield"), with 口(口) (kǒu, "mouth") to convey the notion of 'strength' or 'firmness.' This relates to its origin as the form of 固(固) (gù, "solid").

examples.chinese: 古城非常美丽(古城非常美麗)。
examples.english: The ancient city is very beautiful.

examples.chinese: 他珍藏着一枚古币(他珍藏著一枚古幣)，代表悠久的文化(代表悠久的文化)。
examples.pinyin: Tā zhēncángzhe yī méi gǔ bì, dàibiǎo yōujiǔ de wénhuà.
examples.english: He treasured an ancient coin, representing a long-standing culture.

examples.chinese: 博物馆里展出许多古文物(博物館裡展出許多古文物)。
examples.pinyin: Bówùguǎn lǐ zhǎnchū xǔduō gǔ wénwù.
examples.english: The museum displays many ancient relics.
